displayTitle: Low Hazard Is Earned, Not Granted

beats.narration: A penetrant line looks tame next to a radiography bay — and that's the trap. Its hazards are quiet, cumulative, and yours to control.

payload.title: QUIET HAZARDS
payload.sub: Three families a Level II manages every shift.

beats.narration: Toxicity first, by route. Solvent and oil strip your skin's own oils, so wear the gloves Section 8 names. Wash with soap — never solvent.
Flammability turns on flash point. Qualified penetrants flash above two hundred Fahrenheit, so the open tank is safe. The aerosol removers are the fire risk.

payload.heading: Flash point sets the rules
payload.note: PENETRANT FLASHES HIGH · AEROSOLS FLASH AT ROOM TEMP

beats.narration: Then the lamp. Two non-negotiables: never run a mercury lamp with a cracked or missing filter, and never look into the beam.

chips.s: Blocks bulb's raw UV-B and UV-C

beats.narration: Every control traces to one document — the sixteen-section safety data sheet. Section eight for gloves, nine for flash point.

payload.sub: Sixteen sections, same order, every manufacturer.
